role: A React overlay surface (app-root sibling modal) that lists live sessions, lets the operator filter and drill into one, and renders the selected session via a DNA archetype card plus recall-lens summaries.
capabilities: Closes via scrim click, close button, or Escape key
Client-side filters the roster by title/name/cwd/state
Caps the displayed roster to 60 matches
Selects a session to drill into detail
Navigates back from detail to list
Mounts DNA's `session-card` archetype into a ref for the selected session
Fetches `/dna/layouts.json` to resolve the session-card archetype once
Renders recall-lens sections (`open_loops`, `catch_up`) capped at 8 items
Displays loading and error states with a retry action
substance_why: The file implements a complete overlay workflow: event-driven open/close, async data loading, client-side filtering, selection navigation, DNA card mounting, and recall-lens rendering with loading/error/empty states.
interactions_present: `sessions:open` window event opens the overlay and resets the filter
Escape key deselects the current session or closes the overlay
Back button returns from detail to list
Session row click selects that session
Filter input updates the roster query on every change
interactions_missing: Lens items are displayed but not clickable or actionable
No actions on a selected session (resume, kill, open in editor, etc.)
No empty-state message when the roster is empty and not loading
No sorting controls for the session list
No pagination or "show more" beyond the hard 60-item cap
No focus trap or focus management for the modal
No keyboard navigation within the roster list
No handling/feedback when the selected session id is not found in the roster
Entry verb/chrome is explicitly deferred to DNA design lane
performance_risks: Filter recomputes `toLowerCase()` and scans the full `sessions` array on every render
`sessions.find()` is run multiple times per render and in the card-mount effect
DNA card is rebuilt from scratch on every selection change with no reuse/transition
If `/dna/layouts.json` fetch fails, it will be retried on every subsequent selection until an archetype is cached
Unbounded `sessions` array from the store could bloat memory and render time before the 60-item slice
reuse_why: Keep the session-drill concept, overlay lifecycle, and roster/filter interactions, but rebuild the imperative DNA DOM mounting into a declarative component model and split the list/detail/lens concerns.
decomposition_hints: Split the overlay shell (scrim, dialog, ARIA, close/back controls) from the content panes
Split the roster list into its own component: filter input + virtualized/scrolled list + empty/no-match states
Split the detail view into a DNA-card mount wrapper and a recall-lens renderer
Extract the DNA archetype resolution and mounting into a reusable hook/component
Move the 60-item cap and filter logic into a derived selector or hook
